manuscript_profile.title: Cartel Babies
manuscript_profile.target_audience: Adult readers of literary suspense, borderlands crime fiction, character-driven thrillers, and morally complex family dramas

required_public_strings: Adult readers of literary suspense, borderlands crime fiction, character-driven thrillers, and morally complex family dramas
Literary Thriller / International Crime / Borderlands Fiction
Make final protection sequence procedurally resistant
Must detect Benjamin as dual-POV co-protagonist.
Must detect table tennis as relational bridge activity.
Fails if treated as generic cartel thriller.
Author retains ownership of manuscript content
optional_public_strings: The novel contains a cartel-captivity hook, Benjamin search lane, and Paolito/Paul chosen-family payoff.
Market framing can scatter across cartel, social issue, rescue, queer relationship, and witness-protection shelves.
Define the public-facing premise around chosen family under cartel sovereignty, with cartel survival as the engine and Paolito/Paul as the emotional hinge.
Readers and agents understand the book's unique promise quickly.
Do not flatten the novel into child-rescue sentiment or generic cartel action.
Strong abduction, camp adaptation, and extraction momentum.
Benjamin repetition and final administrative smoothness soften drive.
source_only_strings: A Canadian expatriate abducted by cartel operatives in Sinaloa is pulled into a hidden camp where survival depends on tactical observation, emotional discipline, and dangerous usefulness.
